title: Force unwrap of client.user! in FilesController without guard — potential crash from unauthenticated request
description: FilesController methods (listDirectory, getInfo, createDirectory, setType, setPermissions, delete, move, subscribeDirectory, unsubscribeDirectory) all use client.user! (force unwrap) without first checking that client.user is non-nil. While the ServerController.receiveMessage state machine should prevent unauthenticated clients from reaching these handlers, the guard is not in the handlers themselves. The state machine at ServerController line 448-464 only blocks messages if client.state is CONNECTED or GAVE_CLIENT_INFO, but does not explicitly require LOGGED_IN for all file operations. If a logic error or race condition allows a message to reach these handlers with client.user == nil, the server will crash. There are 25+ instances of client.user! in FilesController.swift alone.
recommendation: Add 'guard let user = client.user else { return }' at the top of each public handler in FilesController, matching the pattern used in receiveDownloadFile and receiveUploadFile. Replace all client.user! with the guarded local variable.
